[[Willie McLean (rugby league)|Willie McLean]] (brother-in-law) }} }} '''Alexander Reremorehu Chan''' (born 22 December 1974) is a [[New Zealand]] former professional [[rugby league]] footballer and coach. Chan played for the [[Catalans Dragons]] of [[Super League]], [[Melbourne Storm]], [[Parramatta Eels]] and [[Northern Eagles]] in the [[NRL]]. A [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand international]] representative forward, Chan had also previously played for the [[Bay of Plenty Stags]] in New Zealand,''Lion Red Rugby League Annual 1994'', [[New Zealand Rugby Football League]], 1994. p.136 and has also played for the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|Aotearoa Māori team]]. ==Background== Chan is of Chinese-Māori descent.{{Cite web |url=http://www.hreoc.gov.au/racial_discrimination/voices/nrlstars/civoniceva_chan.html |title=Human Rights and Equal Opportunity Commission Website |access-date=26 September 2012 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110414010247/http://www.hreoc.gov.au/racial_discrimination/voices/nrlstars/civoniceva_chan.html |archive-date=14 April 2011 |url-status=dead }} His sons [[Tiaki Chan]] and [[Joe Chan]] also played for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]].{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/catalans-vs-warrington/report/55548|title=Catalans Dragons 6–16 Warrington Wolves: Clinical Wolves move into Challenge Cup semi-finals|publisher=Sky Sports|date=7 May 2021|accessdate=8 May 2021}} ==Early career== Chan started playing football at age six with Taupo United rugby union club, playing rugby league with Taupa Hawks from age 10.{{cite web |title=Alex Chan Profile |url=http://www.melbournestorm.com.au/player_profile.php?id=26 |website=melbournestorm.com.au |access-date=20 September 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20050111215439/http://www.melbournestorm.com.au/player_profile.php?id=26 |archive-date=11 January 2005}} He was educated at St Stephen's boarding school near [[Auckland]], and was selected in both junior rugby league and rugby union representative teams. After leaving school, Chan played two seasons with Taupo Broncos. ==Playing career== Moving to Sydney to play professional rugby league, he joined the [[North Sydney Bears]]. He played reserve grade for Norths in 1995–96, crossing to [[Western Suburbs Magpies]] for the 1997 season. Chan represented New Zealand at the 1997 [[Rugby League World Sevens]]. Leaving Wests, Chan joined [[Wentworthville Magpies]], winning [[Ron Massey Cup|Metro Cup]] premierships in 1998 and 1999. Chan then signed with [[National Rugby League|NRL]] club [[Northern Eagles]], making his first grade debut in round 15 2000 against the [[New Zealand Warriors]]. Chan played at fullback for the Māori in 1999 and earlier represented the team at the [[1994 Pacific Cup]].[https://news.bbc.co.uk/1/hi/sport/rugby_league/505826.stm GB have Long to thank] ''BBC Sport'', 5 November 1999{{cite book |last =John Coffey, Bernie Wood |author-link = |title = 100 years: Māori rugby league, 1908–2008 |publisher =Huia Publishers |year = 2008 |location = |pages = 282 |url = https://books.google.com/books?id=nklWo8vw-iIC |doi = |id = |isbn =978-1-86969-331-2}} Chan was selected for the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|Aotearoa Māori side]] at the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|2000 World Cup]] but did not play a match. After the 2000 season, Chan was a regular first grade player for the [[Parramatta Eels]] from 2001 to 2003, coming from the interchange bench in their [[2001 NRL grand final]] loss to the [[Newcastle Knights]]. While with the Eels, he was hampered by injuries, suffering three broken arms in two years limiting him to 26 appearances. Chan played for [[Melbourne Storm]] during the [[2004 Melbourne Storm season|2004]] and [[2005 Melbourne Storm season|2005]] seasons where he was suspended three times by the judiciary for high tackles. During round 20 of the [[2004 NRL season]], Chan was charged and later suspended for four weeks after a reckless high tackle on Parramatta player [[Nathan Hindmarsh]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.theage.com.au/articles/2004/07/26/1090693895785.html?from=storylhs|title=Storm undermanned after Chan is charged|date=27 July 2004}} Granted an early release from his contract in Melbourne,{{cite web |title=Alex Chan Profile |url=http://www.melbournestorm.com.au/default.asp?sec=2&ssec=2&pid=1&prid=1&yr=2005 |website=melbournestorm.com.au |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20051102195852/http://www.melbournestorm.com.au/default.asp?sec=2&ssec=2&pid=1&prid=1&yr=2005 |archive-date=2 November 2005}} Chan finished his professional career at the end of the 2008 [[Super League XIII|Super League]] season, after making 86 appearances for [[Catalans Dragons]], including in the [[2007 Challenge Cup Final]]. ==Coaching== Chan returned to the [[Wentworthville Magpies]] as coach of the [[Sydney Shield]] team, guiding the team to a premiership in 2015.{{cite web |title=Tears of joy for coach Alex Chan |url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2015/09/28/tears-of-joy-for-coach-alex-chan/ |website=nswrl.com.au |access-date=21 September 2021 |date=28 September 2015}} He also coached the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] for two matches 2015–16, with one win; 30–20 [[Lebanon national rugby league team|Lebanon]] and one loss; 8–28 [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]]. ==References== {{reflist|30em}} ==External links== *[https://web.archive.org/web/20080919182604/http://www.superleague.co.uk/profile.php?id=1095 Super League profile] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20051102195852/http://www.melbournestorm.com.au/default.asp?sec=2&ssec=2&pid=1&prid=1&yr=2005 Alex Chan Official Player Profile] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20051029210552/http://www.nrl.com/myclub/players.cfm?TeamID=5&PlayerID=470 Alex Chan NRL Player Profile] {{New Zealand Maori squad 2000 Rugby League World Cup}} {{New Zealand 2004 Rugby League Tri Nations squad}} {{Cook Islands national rugby league team coaches}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Chan, Alex}} [[Category:1974 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Bay of Plenty rugby league team players]] [[Category:Catalans Dragons players]] [[Category:Cook Islands national rugby league team coaches]] [[Category:Junior Kiwis players]] [[Category:Melbourne Storm players]] [[Category:New Zealand Māori rugby league players]] [[Category:New Zealand Māori rugby league team players]] [[Category:New Zealand national rugby league team players]] [[Category:New Zealand people of Chinese descent]] [[Category:Sportspeople of Chinese descent]] [[Category:New Zealand rugby league coaches]] [[Category:New Zealand rugby league players]] [[Category:Northern Eagles players]] [[Category:Parramatta Eels players]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Auckland]] [[Category:Rugby league props]] [[Category:New Zealand expatriate rugby league players in France]] [[Category:New Zealand expatriate rugby league players in Australia]] [[Category:Expatriate rugby league players in France]] [[Category:People educated at St Stephen's School, Bombay Hills]]
